title: Carrier Performance Scoring & Auto-Selection
description: Build a scoring model using historical carrier delivery data to rank carriers by lane, cost, and reliability.
roi_basis: Based on win-002: 12% average per-shipment carrier cost reduction across 150,000 annual shipments. Maturity gap of 0.0 points from engagement baseline -- carrier scoring ROI realises within 6 months when shipment history and carrier invoice data are already centralised.
rag_benchmark: win-002: 12% average cost reduction across 150K shipments
why_this_company: Classified as LOW_HANGING_FRUIT because CargoLogik's composite maturity score of 2.0 (Developing) falls within the 0-2.5 range where proven solutions require minimal change management. BigQuery data lake with Airflow orchestration (sig-001, raw evidence: 'BigQuery data lake with Airflow orchestration') already centralises carrier and shipment data. High-volume data pipeline processing 50M+ events daily (sig-003, raw evidence: 'processing 50M+ events daily') provides the training data density needed for a scoring model without additional instrumentation. Logistics industry with 2M+ monthly shipments (sig-006, raw evidence: '2M+ shipments monthly across 200+ logistics customers') aligns directly with win-002's B2B logistics carrier scoring precedent. The 2.0 score confirms this company is not yet running ML models but has the data foundation -- LOW_HANGING_FRUIT tier is correct because infrastructure readiness is high and ML complexity is low.

data_inputs: Shipment performance history from BigQuery (18 months)
data_flow.model_approach: Weighted scoring model ranking carriers per lane and weight class
data_flow.output_consumer: Operations team via carrier recommendation dashboard
data_flow.feedback_loop: Monthly model refresh with new delivery performance data
data_flow.value_measurement: Per-shipment cost tracked monthly vs 6-month pre-deployment baseline

title: Predictive Route Optimization
description: ML-based route optimization using GPS telemetry and historical shipment data to reduce fuel costs and improve on-time delivery.
roi_estimate: 12-16% fuel cost reduction, estimated $250K-$350K annual savings
roi_basis: Based on win-001: 14% fuel cost reduction on $2.1M annual fleet spend. Maturity gap of 0.0 points from engagement baseline -- route optimisation ROI is achievable in 3-5 months with existing GPS telemetry and TMS API access. Adjusted for CargoLogik's scale of 2M+ monthly shipments.
rag_benchmark: win-001: 14% fuel cost reduction ($294K annual savings)
why_this_company: Classified as MEDIUM_SOLUTION because CargoLogik's composite maturity score of 2.0 (Developing) sits within the 2.0-3.5 range for this tier, and route optimization requires building a new ML model rather than applying an existing tool -- that process change places it above LOW_HANGING_FRUIT. Airflow orchestration (sig-002, raw evidence: 'BigQuery data lake with Airflow orchestration') provides an ETL pipeline capable of feeding a training workflow. Kubernetes infrastructure with CI/CD (sig-005, raw evidence: 'Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD') enables containerised model serving without new infrastructure. High event volume of 50M+ events daily (sig-003, raw evidence: 'processing 50M+ events daily') supplies sufficient training samples for XGBoost route scoring. The 2.0 score confirms data readiness but limited ML capability -- MEDIUM_SOLUTION is correct because the company needs to hire or upskill before deployment, which is the distinguishing factor from LOW_HANGING_FRUIT.

data_flow.model_approach: XGBoost regression for route scoring, adapted from win-001 approach
data_flow.output_consumer: Dispatchers via route planning interface integrated with existing TMS
data_flow.feedback_loop: Weekly retraining on actual vs predicted delivery times
data_flow.value_measurement: Monthly fuel cost compared to same period prior year, adjusted for fuel price index

title: Demand Forecasting for Warehouse Capacity Planning
description: Time-series forecasting to predict shipping volume by customer and region, enabling proactive warehouse staffing.
roi_estimate: Projected 15-25% reduction in warehouse labor cost variance
roi_basis: Projected conservative estimate. No direct Tenex precedent in this company's exact configuration.
why_this_company: Classified as HARD_EXPERIMENT because this use case falls outside the typical composite_score range of 3.0-5.0 for this tier -- CargoLogik scores 2.0 (Developing), which is below the 3.0 threshold. That is precisely why confidence is 0.52 (lower than the other tiers) and this is positioned as experimental. Despite the maturity gap, three signals indicate the data conditions are present: high data volume of 50M+ events daily (sig-003, raw evidence: 'processing 50M+ events daily') across 200+ customers (sig-007, raw evidence: '200+ customers') creates seasonal patterns that forecasting models can learn; mid-market logistics scale (sig-006, raw evidence: '2M+ shipments monthly across 200+ logistics customers') matches the order-of-magnitude feasibility for time-series forecasting. HARD_EXPERIMENT is chosen over MEDIUM_SOLUTION because there is no Tenex win precedent for this exact use case, the required ML capability (time-series forecasting) exceeds what a 2.0-maturity company can absorb without significant investment, and warehouse staffing integration adds operational complexity that has not been piloted.

data_flow.output_consumer: Warehouse managers via weekly capacity planning dashboard
data_flow.feedback_loop: Monthly accuracy review comparing predicted vs actual volumes
data_flow.value_measurement: Quarterly warehouse labor cost variance vs prior year
